# PiggyHub

> Family financial education app — parents manage chores and allowances while kids learn to Spend, Save, and Give.

PiggyHub is a web application for families with children. Every dollar a child earns through chores or allowances is automatically split across three buckets — **Spend**, **Save**, and **Give** — teaching healthy money habits from an early age. Parents have a full management dashboard; children get a simplified, colorful kid-friendly view of their own balances and progress.

## Who it's for

- **Parents** who want a structured, trackable system for chores and allowances
- **Children ages 5–16** learning about money, saving, and generosity
- **Families** building long-term financial literacy habits together

## Core features

- **Three-bucket system**: Every dollar earned splits into Spend, Save, and Give at a ratio parents configure per child
- **Chore engine**: Parents assign chores with dollar values; kids mark them done; parents approve
- **Allowances**: Scheduled (weekly / biweekly / monthly) with one-click payment
- **Savings goals**: Kids create goals (bike, game, trip, gift…) with target amounts and dates; they contribute from any bucket and watch a progress bar fill up
- **Parent savings match**: Parents can match a percentage of what kids put into their Save bucket to incentivize saving
- **Kid view**: Age-appropriate dashboard with large colorful bucket cards, goal progress, chore list, badges, and streaks
- **Badges & streaks**: Achievement system rewards consistent chore completion
- **Security**: Row-level security via Supabase — parents only see their own family's data

## Technology

Built with React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite, and Supabase (Postgres + Auth + RLS).

## Key URLs

- App: https://piggy.joshdormont.com
- Sign up: https://piggy.joshdormont.com (unauthenticated users are redirected to sign-in/sign-up)
